meta_title: For Founders: analytics setup to Solve tutorial hell (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome tutorial hell. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Analytics Setup to Solve Tutorial Hell
content.seo_description: Stop drifting in tutorial hell. Use this expert-led checklist to implement analytics, track user behavior, and validate your SaaS idea using Antigravity.
content.answer_block: Tutorial hell ends when you stop watching and start measuring. By integrating lightweight analytics early, you transform your MVP into a feedback loop. Using Antigravity and Claude Code, you can instrument your core user flows in minutes, allowing data—not guesswork—to dictate your product's roadmap and monetization strategy.
content.intro:
You have spent weeks following tutorials, yet your SaaS remains a ghost town. You are trapped in the endless loop of 'learning' while your competitors are already capturing market share. The difference between a hobbyist and a founder isn't the quality of code; it's the ability to see what users actually do when they land on your page.

Today, we break the cycle. We are moving beyond the tutorial interface and into the realm of real-world business intelligence. By instrumenting your application with surgical precision, you will identify exactly where users drop off, what features they value, and why they aren't converting. It is time to treat your code as a business asset.

steps.title: Define Your North Star Metric
steps.description: Before writing code, identify the one action that signals value delivery. For a SaaS, this is usually 'Account Created' or 'First AI Generation'.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current SaaS flow and suggest the three most critical events to track for user activation and retention.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /ask 'Identify the primary activation event in my codebase and outline the logic to track it.'

steps.title: Initialize Analytics Middleware
steps.description: Create a centralized wrapper for your analytics events to avoid vendor lock-in and keep your codebase clean.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a robust, TypeScript-based analytics provider pattern that allows me to swap between PostHog and Mixpanel without changing application code.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /run 'Create a new file src/lib/analytics.ts implementing a singleton pattern for event tracking.'

steps.title: Instrument the Conversion Funnel
steps.description: Add tracking events to your registration and checkout flows to measure the friction points in your onboarding.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Inject event tracking calls into my signup and payment handler functions, ensuring user IDs are captured correctly.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /fix 'Add analytics.track("signup_initiated") to the auth service and capture the user ID.'

steps.title: Implement Session Replay
steps.description: Visual proof beats logs. Install session replay to watch how users interact with your UI in real-time.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide the configuration code to integrate session recording into my React/Next.js stack, ensuring I filter out sensitive PII fields.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /run 'Install the session replay SDK and wrap the root layout component with the provider.'

steps.title: Automate Weekly Insight Reports
steps.description: Don't just collect data; act on it. Set up automated summaries of your funnel performance.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a script that queries my analytics API for the last 7 days of user activity and formats it into a summary for my Slack channel.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /run 'Create a cron job in scripts/report.ts that fetches analytics data and logs the conversion rate.'

pro_tips: Never track everything; track only what informs a business decision. Over-tracking leads to data paralysis.
Always pseudonymize user IDs. Use database UUIDs rather than emails to comply with GDPR/CCPA from day one.
Monitor your 'Time to First Value' (TTFV) as your primary metric; if it's over 60 seconds, you are losing money.
Use feature flags to toggle analytics intensity during high-traffic periods to save on event ingestion costs.
Validate your tracking in staging before production; bad data is worse than no data.
content.next_steps: Once your analytics are live, spend 48 hours observing user behavior. Identify the biggest drop-off point in your funnel and perform a single A/B test to improve it. Iterate based on real data, not intuition.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer a student; you are a builder with a feedback loop. Stop guessing what your users want and start measuring it. The market is waiting for your solution. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
